intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called "Greeneochloa expansa." However, I found information on "Greeneochloa expansa" is not a real plant, but I did find Greeneochloa expansa is a synonym for the plant  "Kochia expansa" also known as Kochia. 

Kochia (Kochia scoparia) is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antidiabetic activities. The bioactive constituents of Kochia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Kochia is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
